teachers.name: Huanglong Huinan
teachers.notes: Editorial bridge for Eisai's Huanglong lineage when the direct teacher Xuan Huaichang is not yet in the canonical corpus.

teachers.name: Yasutani Hakuun
teachers.notes: Wikipedia (citing the White Plum Asanga founder page) records that Maezumi completed kōan study under Yasutani and received his inka (dharma transmission) in 1970. Marked primary so the Sōtō-filtered /lineage view surfaces the Sanbō-Zen branch that actually transmits into Maezumi; his Sōtō shiho from Baian Hakujun Kuroda remains the family lineage.

teachers.name: Shunryu Suzuki
teachers.notes: Katagiri's Minnesota Zen Center biography records that he assisted Shunryu Suzuki at San Francisco Zen Center before founding his own community in Minnesota.

teachers.name: Shunryu Suzuki
teachers.notes: Kobun's biography identifies Shunryu Suzuki as the decisive later influence who brought him to Tassajara in 1967.

teachers.name: Keizan Jokin
teachers.notes: The Order of Buddhist Contemplatives presents Keido Chisan Koho Zenji within the transmitted Soto lineage that continues through Keizan, Dogen, Bodhidharma, and Shakyamuni Buddha. This is a reviewed backbone anchor pending a fuller direct Japanese succession chain.

teachers.name: Keido Chisan
teachers.notes: The Order of Buddhist Contemplatives identifies Keido Chisan Koho Zenji as Jiyu-Kennett's Japanese Soto teacher and transmitter.

teachers.name: Bodhidharma
teachers.notes: Terebess describes Mahasattva Fu as a younger contemporary and important precursor in early Zen traditions alongside Bodhidharma; this is an editorial anchor into the early Zen graph, not a claim of formal discipleship.
